title: centaurea × bilbilitana
intro: Centaurea × bilbilitana, also known as the Aragonese knapweed or Bilbilitana knapweed, is a hybrid plant species in the Asteraceae family, derived from the cross between Centaurea cuneifolia and Centaurea × moncktonii. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Centaurea × bilbilitana include reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and promoting wound healing, as well as possibly reducing the risk of certain diseases due to its antioxidant properties. Centaurea × bilbilitana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
